headings.usage: Page titles, Section headers, Signal status

layout_strategy.spacing: space-y-8 or gap-8 (Generous spacing for clarity)

header.style: sticky top-0 z-50 w-full border-b border-white/10 bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-xl supports-[backdrop-filter]:bg-black/20

cards.style: rounded-xl border border-white/10 bg-zinc-900/40 backdrop-blur-sm transition-all duration-300 hover:border-white/20

buttons.primary: bg-indigo-600 text-white hover:bg-indigo-500 shadow-lg shadow-indigo-500/20 transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: bg-zinc-800 text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-700 hover:text-white border border-zinc-700

badges.style: inline-flex items-center rounded-full px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium ring-1 ring-inset

tables.row_style: group hover:bg-zinc-800/50 transition-colors border-b border-zinc-800 last:border-0

effects.gradient_border: bg-gradient-to-r from-transparent via-indigo-500 to-transparent h-[1px] w-full

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) against the dark background. Use lighter grays (zinc-300/400) for secondary text, not dark grays.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Tailwind Config: Add 'Manrope', 'IBM Plex Sans', and 'JetBrains Mono' to font families. Add custom colors for signals.
2. Install Dependencies: 'lucide-react' for icons, 'framer-motion' for animations, 'clsx' and 'tailwind-merge' for class handling.
3. Build Layout: Create a sticky header with glass effect. Use a responsive grid for the main dashboard.
4. AI Card: This is the centerpiece. Use the 'tracing beam' or a glowing border effect to highlight the daily signal.
5. Calendar: Build a dense data table. Do not use standard Shadcn table if it's too spacious. Create a custom compact row component.
6. Theme: FORCE Dark Mode. Do not implement a light mode toggle. The persona is 'Professional Trader'.
7. Data Visualization: Use simple progress bars or radial dials for 'Probability Scores'. Avoid complex D3 charts if unnecessary.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: - You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
- Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
- Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
- Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
- Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
- You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
- You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
- Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
- NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
- Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
- Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
- Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
- Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
- Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
- Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
- Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
